package handler

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
	"log"
	"net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for custom_recovery_logic_repeated_across_handlers.
// Rule intent: Repeated recover or panic-to-response logic inside handlers instead of centralized recovery middleware.
type Order struct {
	ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
}

type User struct {
	ID   string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
	Name string `gorm:"column:name" json:"name"`
}

func GetOrderHandler(c *gin.Context, db *gorm.DB) {
	defer func() {
		if r := recover(); r != nil {
			log.Printf("recovered from panic in GetOrderHandler: %v", r)
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "internal server error"})
		}
	}()

	var order Order
	db.First(&order, "id = ?", c.Param("id"))
	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, order)
}

func CreateUserHandler(c *gin.Context, db *gorm.DB) {
	defer func() {
		if r := recover(); r != nil {
			log.Printf("recovered from panic in CreateUserHandler: %v", r)
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "internal server error"})
		}
	}()

	var user User
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&user); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}
	db.Create(&user)
	c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, user)
}

func DeleteOrderHandler(c *gin.Context, db *gorm.DB) {
	defer func() {
		if r := recover(); r != nil {
			log.Printf("recovered from panic in DeleteOrderHandler: %v", r)
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "internal server error"})
		}
	}()

	db.Delete(&Order{}, "id = ?", c.Param("id"))
	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"deleted": true})
}
